steps.title: Spot the introductory phrase boundary
steps.content: The blank appears between an introductory prepositional phrase (“With almost two millennia of climate data in its tree”) and the main clause that begins with “a single tree like this.”

steps.title: Apply the introductory phrase punctuation rule
steps.content:
A comma must separate an introductory prepositional phrase from the main clause.

Choice B correctly places the comma after “rings.” The other choices either use “and” to create an incorrect compound (A), place a period that breaks the sentence (C), or misuse a semicolon before a non-independent clause (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
